name: Notification History Belongs to the Shell
designPattern: Shell-Owned Notification Center / Reviewable Feedback Surface
requirements: Existing notification stack/history substrate in notification.ts
Tests ARE the Spec roadmap principle

userStory.asA: user of an interactive framed Bijou app
userStory.iWant: one shell-owned place to review current and recent notifications
userStory.soThat: important feedback is recoverable after transient toasts disappear and I do not have to guess where durable notice review lives

acceptanceCriteria: createFramedApp() can expose a standard shell-owned notification center without each app inventing its own history modal
The notification center is anchored to the right edge and keeps the current page visible behind it
The notification center is backed by the existing notification archive/history model instead of introducing a second store
The shell exposes a standard entry path for notification review through a shell binding and a command-palette command
The shell footer can surface a subtle notification/inbox cue without dumping the full archive state into the chrome
The notification center owns keyboard and mouse input while open instead of leaking interaction through to the underlying page
The notification center can scroll independently when the list of items exceeds the drawer height
The notification center supports a simple filter model aligned with the existing notification system: `ALL`, `ACTIONABLE`, `ERROR`, `WARNING`, `SUCCESS`, `INFO`
Archived notifications may expose a follow-up action only when the app provides one explicitly; the shell does not invent replay semantics by default
A framed app with notification state can adopt the shell-owned center with minimal custom code, and frame-managed runtime notifications can use the same review surface
backwardCompatibility: This is additive shell behavior. Existing framed apps remain valid without providing a notification center. The first slice adds a standard review container and entry points, not a breaking change to notification records or runtime routing.

inScope: Standard shell entry points for notification review
Scroll and filter behavior inside the center
Reuse of the existing notification history substrate
One framed proving surface plus runtime-notification compatibility
outScope: Automatic replay of archived notification actions without explicit app opt-in

testPlan.goldenPath: Write failing app-frame tests that prove a right-edge notification center can open from standard shell entry points, owns keyboard and mouse input while open, scrolls independently, cycles filters, and exposes a footer cue. Then add a framed notifications-lab regression proving archived items render through the shared shell container rather than an app-specific modal.
edgeCases: Active stack empty but archive non-empty
Archive long enough to require scrolling
Compact terminal width with a narrow drawer
Opening help, settings, or the command palette while the notification center is already open
Archived actionable notifications with no explicit activation handler
testPlan.fuzzStress: Not a priority in v0. The main risk is shell coherence, routing precedence, and honest review behavior, not high-throughput message volume.
failureCases: Toasts disappear and there is still no standard place to review them later
The shell opens a notification center that drifts from the real archive/history state
The notification center leaks keyboard or mouse input through to the workspace underneath
Every framed app still needs its own history modal despite the shell feature existing
The footer becomes a noisy second inbox instead of a subtle discoverability cue

estimatedComplexity.linesOfCode: ~220-420 lines across app-frame state, shell bindings, drawer rendering, notification-center routing, and regressions
estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(V) per render and interaction where V is the number of visible notification-center rows; the v0 risk is product coherence and shell routing, not algorithmic complexity
